messages.content:
# Key Discussions from Client Demo (January 28, 2026)

Here are the main topics covered during the Acme Corp client demo:

## 1. Problem Statement
- Acme Corp has **data silos** across marketing, sales, and customer support platforms
- Goal: Track the **customer journey** from first touch to renewal

## 2. Product Demonstration
- **Unified customer profile** aggregating emails, calls, support tickets, and usage data
- Dashboard view showing all touchpoints in one place

## 3. Technical Implementation
- **Data matching**: Deterministic matching (99% accuracy for emails/phones) + probabilistic modeling for anonymous visitors
- **Implementation timeline**: 8-12 weeks total (4 weeks data integration, 4 weeks dashboard customization, plus testing)

## 4. Privacy & Compliance
- GDPR and CCPA compliant
- Built-in consent management
- Configurable data retention policies per region
- Data deletion API for right-to-be-forgotten requests

## 5. Integrations & API
- Full REST and GraphQL API access
- 200+ pre-built connectors (Salesforce, HubSpot, Zendesk, ServiceNow)
- Real-time webhooks for pushing data to internal systems

## 6. Reporting & Mobile
- Drag-and-drop report builder with custom visualizations
- Scheduled automated email reports
- iOS app with offline support and auto-sync

## 7. Training & Support
- In-app guided tours
- Live and recorded training sessions
- 24/7 support team
- ~2 week learning curve for average users

## 8. Pricing
- Approximately **$150,000 annually** (based on users and data volume)
- Includes unlimited API calls and 24/7 support

## 9. Decision Timeline
- Acme Corp plans to decide by **end of Q1** (~60 days)

## 10. Data Safety
- Data portability guarantee (export in standard formats)
- Vendor is Series C funded
- Option for data escrow in contract

---

Is there a specific topic you'd like me to elaborate on?
what are the action items required from Product Demonstration

# Action Items from Product Demonstration

Based on the meeting, the action items that resulted from (or were discussed after) the product demonstration are:

## 1. Prepare Proof of Concept with Sample Data
- **Owner:** Nina (Solutions Engineer)
- **Details:** Nina will prepare a proof of concept demonstrating the platform with sample data from Acme Corp

## 2. Schedule Technical Deep Dive for Next Week
- **Owner:** Kevin (Sales)
- **Details:** Schedule a follow-up session to go deeper into technical details

## 3. Follow Up Tomorrow with Technical Deep Dive Scheduling
- **Owner:** Kevin (Sales)
- **Details:** Kevin will reach out the next day to finalize the technical deep dive appointment

---

These action items were discussed at the end of the demo when Kevin proposed:
> *"We can do a technical deep dive next week if you want. Nina can also prepare a proof of concept with some sample data."*

Robert agreed this would be helpful, confirming these as next steps stemming from the product demonstration.

Would you like more details on any of these action items?

# Key Discussion Points

Based on the meeting on **February 18, 2026**, here are the main topics discussed:

## 1. Current Problems
- **2 million daily active users** with 24-hour JWT token expiry
- **5% of mobile users** experiencing stuck sessions
- **15ms latency** per token validation due to database lookups
- **50,000 validations per minute** at peak times

## 2. Three Options Considered

| Option | Description | Pros | Cons |
|--------|-------------|------|------|
| **Option 1** | Redis caching with JWTs | Fast to implement, <5ms latency, no extra cost | Still needs client-side token refresh |
| **Option 2** | Opaque tokens + session database | Immediate revocation | Complex, 3-4 weeks work, ~$500/month |
| **Option 3** | OAuth 2.0 (Auth0/Okta) | No maintenance, SOC2 compliant | $2,000/month, gives up control |

## 3. Security Considerations
- Database lookups for every token validation create an exploitable bottleneck
- Key rotation without downtime is required
- Third-party providers offer strongest security but add cost

## 4. Decision
The team reached **consensus on Option 1** (Redis caching) as the fastest, most cost-effective solution that can be built upon later if needed.

---

Would you like me to elaborate on any specific aspect?
